sections.paragraph:
τρίμηνος, ον (τρεῖς, μήν; Soph.et al.) of three months as subst. ἡ τρίμηνος (Hdt. 2, 124; Aeschin. 3, 70; PLond I, 18, 10 p. 23 [161 b.c.]; PSI 689, 5; 30. See B-D-F §241, 3: sc. περίοδος) or τὸ τρίμηνον (Polyb. 1, 38, 6; 5, 1, 12; Plut., Crass. 550 [12, 3]; Ptolem. 1, 8, 6.—Doubtful ἐν τριμήνῳ: SIG 527, 114 [perh. 220 b.c.]; 1023, 31; Jos., Ant. 3, 62; διὰ τριμήνου PLond II, 306, 22 p. 119 [II a.d.]; LXX) (a period of) three months τρίμηνον (acc. in answer to the question, how long? 4 Km 24:8; 2 Ch 36:2.—B-D-F §161, 2; Rob. 469–71) for three months Hb 11:23.—DELG s.v. τρεῖς. M-M.

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

et et al. = et alii (and others)

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Aeschin Aeschin , IV b.c.; spurious letters II a.d.—List 5

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

  1. sc. = scilicet (one may understand, supply)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Ptolem Ptolem , math., II a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

perh. perh. = perhaps

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

acc. acc. = accusative

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1015
